title: Conflict Diagnosis and Semantic Resolution · Graph Lens: Conflict Marker Reading
summary: Resolve an integration conflict by reconstructing both intents and verifying behavior rather than deleting markers until Git accepts the file. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git conflict marker reading increment in a conflict reproduction, resolution, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Read conflict markers and diff views while obtaining surrounding base and commit intent.
Inspect unmerged index stages, ours/theirs/base terminology, paths, and operation context before editing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Conflict Marker Reading
steps.instruction:
theory case — Merge conflict in policy.txt has base limit=2, main limit=6, and repair limit=7 plus retry tests. Inspect index stages, reconstruct intent, create a semantic result, and verify before continue.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Conflict Marker Reading contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for read conflict markers and diff views while obtaining surrounding base and commit intent.

content.text: Merge conflict in policy.txt has base limit=2, main limit=6, and repair limit=7 plus retry tests. Inspect index stages, reconstruct intent, create a semantic result, and verify before continue.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Conflict Marker Reading condition decided your prediction.

content.value: gitworkflows Manual — Controls integration, topic branch, merge, release, and distributed maintainer workflow guidance.

steps.title: Bound Conflict Marker Reading
steps.instruction:
theory case — Merge conflict in policy.txt has base limit=2, main limit=2, and repair limit=3 plus retry tests. Inspect index stages, reconstruct intent, create a semantic result, and verify before continue.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Conflict Marker Reading application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for read conflict markers and diff views while obtaining surrounding base and commit intent.

content.text: Read conflict markers and diff views while obtaining surrounding base and commit intent. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Merge conflict in policy.txt has base limit=2, main limit=6, and repair limit=7 plus retry tests. Inspect index stages, reconstruct intent, create a semantic result, and verify before continue. A defensible solution applies Conflict Marker Reading, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a conflict reproduction, resolution, and regression record.

content.code:
base=$(git merge-base main repair)
printf 'base=%s\n' "$base"
git diff "$base"..repair
git ls-files --unmerged
# Resolve intent, stage, test, then continue.
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Choosing both sides line-by-line necessarily preserves both behaviors. This fails the Conflict Marker Reading contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Conflict Marker Reading evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-conflict-marker-reading-klxieh" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Conflict Marker Reading into observable performance evidence.
